Description: The Sustainable Development Report 2025 tracks the performance of all 193 UN Member States on the 17 Sustainable Development Goals.

Title: What is Sustainability (8.5 × 11 in)
Description: Three Dimensions of Sustainability. Although sustainability is linked to the environmental movement, the notion that it is only focused on the environment is a misconception.

Title: Tetra Pak Sustainability Report | Tetra Pak Global
Description: Read our latest sustainability report. In 2025, we achieved key sustainability milestones while pursuing our purpose of protecting what’s good - food, people, and the planet.

Title: Sustainable Development Report 2025
Description: The Sustainable Development Report (SDR) reviews progress made each year on the SDGs since their adoption by all UN Member States in 2015.

Title: ESG (Environmental, Social, & Governance)
Description: ESG is an acronym for Environmental, Social, and Governance. ESG takes the holistic view that sustainability extends beyond just environmental issues.

Title: California Corporate Greenhouse Gas (GHG) Reporting and Climate Related Financial Risk Disclosure Programs
Description: The California Corporate Greenhouse Gas Reporting Program, authorized by Senate Bill (SB) 253 (Wiener, 2023) is being developed by the California Air Resources Board (CARB) and will require business entities formed under the laws of California, the laws of any other state of the United States or the District of Columbia, or under an act of the Congress of the United States, with total annual revenues in excess of one billion dollars ($1,000,000,000) that do business in California (“reporting entities”) to annually disclose their scope 1, 2 and 3 greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions for the prior fiscal year. The Climate Related Financial Risk Disclosure Program authorized by SB 261 (Stern, 2023) also applies to both public and private U.S.

Title: The State of Corporate Sustainability Disclosure 2025
Description: Center for Corporate Environmental Performance The State of Corporate Sustainability Disclosure 2025 This year’s State of Corporate Sustainability Disclosure report reveals growing momentum among S&P 500 companies in climate-related transparency—especially around emissions, net-zero goals, risk reporting, and transition planning—while highlighting persistent gaps in data quality, cost ...

Title: Charting the Change: Disclosure Data Dashboard - CDP
Description: Companies disclosing on all three environmental issues - climate change, deforestation and water security - increased significantly in 2024. More holistic data will empower organizations to better assess the environmental risks, impacts and opportunities in their operations, supply chain, product and service offerings and financial decisions.
